name: checkbox-list
description: A multi-select FORM control: a fieldset of native input[type=checkbox] options sharing one `name`, with an optional search box and a select-all / clear bulk toggle (Filament's CheckboxList; shadcn composes Checkbox + Command/search). Each option row composes the re-forged lievit checkbox primitive via @template.lievit.checkbox(...) with a SIBLING label[for] (the v-next checkbox has no label param). JS-OFF it is a fully accessible native multi-select that POSTs each checked value under the repeated `name` in a plain form (unchecked boxes are simply absent); the search + bulk-toggle UI stays hidden so there are no dead controls. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer reveals the tools and adds client-side filtering + a one-click select-all that fires native change so l:model and forms see it. The native checkboxes stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA APG Checkbox Group: native fieldset/legend group, aria-required on fieldset, role=searchbox on the filter, aria-pressed + dynamic label on the toggle. Plain HTML + Tailwind v4 + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.
docs: Copies the checkbox-list partial to your JTE root at lievit/checkbox-list.jte (reached as @template.lievit.checkbox-list(name=..., options=...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/checkbox-list.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllCheckboxLists() on load (and after any DOM swap). JS-OFF the native checkboxes POST every checked value under the repeated name; the search + select-all controls stay hidden. JS-ON the enhancer reveals them and adds client-side filtering + a bulk toggle whose label swaps between selectAllLabel and clearLabel to reflect the all-checked state. Pass `options` (ordered Map of String to String, value to label) or a `content` slot of hand-authored option markup; `values` lists the initially-checked values; `searchable` / `bulkToggle` opt into the progressive tools; `columns` (1 / 2 / 3) sets the grid; `label` gives a visible legend, `ariaLabel` names the group when no legend is visible; `describedBy` points to a hint/error element; `disabled` / `required` / `invalid` manage group state. Requires the checkbox primitive (each option row) and the icon partial (the search glyph). The source is yours: edit freely.
